---
title: "Age gaps in movie couples across the years"
description: "Scatterplot showing the age gap between the actors playing a romantic relationship on screen. "
date: "2026-03-05"
image: "../images/measures-rodents.jpg"
categories: [R, Tidytuesday, Scatterplot]
toc: true
---

![](../images/measures-rodents.jpg){.project-hero-img fig-alt="Scatterplot showing the age gap between the actors playing a romantic relationship on screen."}

::: {.project-meta}
::: {.meta-item}
📅 March 2026
:::
::: {.meta-item}
🛠️ R, ggplot2, dplyr
:::
::: {.meta-item}
📊 [Data source](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2023/2023-02-14/readme.md)
<!-- ::: -->
<!-- ::: {.meta-item} -->
<!-- 💻 [Code on GitHub](https://github.com/yourusername/repo) -->
:::
:::

## What & Why

I was curious about the age gap evolution through time wishing that they would narrow the closer we are to today. 
The message as always ended up being more complicated. The maximum age gap has been decreasing since the early
2000s

## Approach

The basic plot is a relatively straight forward scatterplot. The magic happened 
when checking actors that appear multiple times in the dataset and see the age of
all their onscreen partners at the time of each of their film. The using color for
showing each of the actors and text to give an explanation to the line joining the dots.
